title: Point-in-time prediction snapshot file
description: A snapshot file is an ARRAY of rows written by scripts/harness/snapshot.py to data/snapshots/. Each row is a prediction frozen before its event, later resolved. The honesty invariant (see scripts/harness/honesty.py) is enforced in code, not fully expressible in JSON Schema: estimate=true => brier/log_loss absent/null; estimate=false & resolved=true => brier/log_loss present.

event_id.description: Stable id of the game or player-week.

as_of_utc.description: Information cutoff (<= kickoff) — the leak-safety anchor.

probs.description: Probability vector (games). Mutually exclusive with point.

point.description: Point estimate (player points). Mutually exclusive with probs.

estimate.description: true => NOT a measurement; scores must be absent/null.

resolved.description: true once the event has finished and actual is known.

actual.description: Realized outcome: an outcome index (int) for probs rows, or a value for point rows. Untyped on purpose.
